description: Set up Firebase Storage, create imageStorageService.js module, and rewrite all image flows in App.jsx to use Firebase Storage URLs instead of base64 data
steps: Step 1 - Add Firebase Storage to firebase.js: Import `getStorage` from 'firebase/storage'. Initialize the storage instance alongside db. Export `storage` alongside existing exports (app, db, isFirebaseConfigured).

Step 2 - Create src/imageStorageService.js: A new module with these functions: (a) `uploadImage(file, projectId, workspaceId, imageId)` - uploads to path `images/{projectId}/{workspaceId}/{imageId}`, returns download URL via getDownloadURL. (b) `deleteImage(projectId, workspaceId, imageId)` - deletes the file at that storage path. (c) `deleteWorkspaceImages(projectId, workspaceId, imageIds)` - batch deletes all images for a workspace. All functions should gracefully handle errors (log warning, don't throw). Import ref, uploadBytes, getDownloadURL, deleteObject from 'firebase/storage'. Check isFirebaseConfigured() before operations. Accept raw File or Blob objects.

Step 3 - Rewrite handleCanvasImageDrop in App.jsx (line ~3824): Remove FileReader/canvas/toDataURL logic. Instead: (a) generate imageId upfront, (b) call uploadImage(file, activeProjectId, activeTab, imageId), (c) create image object with `url` field (not `src`) containing the download URL, (d) still calculate display dimensions from loaded Image element for width/height, (e) still detect group membership. Mark image as loading during upload with a temporary placeholder if needed.

Step 4 - Rewrite the inline upload button at line ~5007: Same pattern as step 3. The inline `<input type='file'>` onChange currently has the full base64 logic inline. Refactor to call a shared upload function or replicate the Storage upload pattern.
Step 5 - Update image rendering JSX (line ~5744): Change `

Step 6 - Rewrite deleteImage function (line ~3904): After removing the image from workspace data, also call imageStorageService.deleteImage(activeProjectId, activeTab, imgId) to remove the file from Firebase Storage. Also handle the multi-select delete at line ~6807 similarly.

Step 7 - Update duplicateWorkspace (line ~2240): The current code creates new image IDs but copies all data. With URL-based images, the `newImages` mapping should keep the same `url` (shared Storage file) but get new id and workspaceId. No file copying needed.
Step 8 - Update multi-select Duplicate button (line ~6809): Same pattern - when duplicating selected images, keep the same `url` but assign new id. No storage file copy.

Step 9 - Update export functions: In exportSelectedNodes (line ~2985), images are NOT currently included in partial exports (only nodes/edges/groups). Leave this as-is. In exportAllData (line ~2791), workspace data includes images array which will now contain URLs instead of base64 - this is fine, URLs are portable. For handleImport and importAllData, images imported from old backups may contain `src` (base64) - the rendering already handles this via `img.url || img.src` from step 5.

Step 10 - Ensure undo/redo compatibility: The takeSnapshot/undo/redo system stores full workspace state snapshots. Since image objects now just have a `url` string instead of base64 `src`, undo/redo automatically works - restoring a snapshot restores the URL references. No code change needed but verify the image object shape is consistent.

Step 11 - Handle workspace deletion: When a workspace is deleted (find deleteWorkspace logic), call deleteWorkspaceImages to clean up all Storage files for that workspace. When a project is deleted, iterate all workspace image arrays and delete their Storage files.

Step 12 - Remove all remaining base64 image creation code: Ensure no path in App.jsx creates images with `src: base64Data` for canvas images. The project thumbnail upload (line ~5007 area) is a DIFFERENT feature (project thumbnails) - do NOT change that, it uses setProjectThumbnailInput and is unrelated to canvas images.
acceptance_criteria: firebase.js exports a `storage` instance initialized from getStorage(app)
src/imageStorageService.js exists with uploadImage, deleteImage, deleteWorkspaceImages functions
handleCanvasImageDrop uploads file to Firebase Storage and stores URL in image object
The toolbar Upload Image button uses the same Storage upload pattern
Image rendering uses `img.url || img.src` for backward compatibility
deleteImage removes file from Firebase Storage in addition to workspace data
Workspace duplication reuses existing URLs without copying files
The project thumbnail upload (setProjectThumbnailInput) is NOT modified
npm run build succeeds with no errors
verification: Run `npm run build` in /projects/sandbox/ClearMind and confirm it succeeds
Verify firebase.js exports storage by reading the file
Verify imageStorageService.js exists and exports the required functions
Verify App.jsx no longer contains toDataURL or readAsDataURL for canvas image uploads (project thumbnail upload is exempt)
findings: Step 4 (toolbar upload button) was not applicable because the only file input in the ~5007 area is the project thumbnail upload which must not be modified. The canvas image upload path is exclusively via drag-and-drop (handleCanvasImageDrop). Steps 7, 8, 9, 10 required no code changes as existing patterns already support URL-based images correctly.
